description: A paginated collection of monitored entities.
name: EntityCollection

nextPageKey.description: Cursor for the next page of results. Null if no more pages.

totalCount.description: The total number of entities matching the query.

pageSize.description: The number of results returned on this page.

entities.description: The list of entities on this page.

items.description: Represents a monitored entity in Dynatrace. Entities are the components of your monitored environment such as services, hosts, processes, and applications. Each entity has a unique ID, a type, properties, tags, and relationships to other entities.

entityId.description: The unique identifier of the entity in the format TYPE-HEXADECIMALID, e.g., SERVICE-1234567890ABCDEF.

displayName.description: The human-readable display name of the entity.

type.description: The type of the entity, e.g., SERVICE, HOST, PROCESS_GROUP, APPLICATION, SYNTHETIC_TEST.

firstSeenTms.description: The Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the entity was first seen.

lastSeenTms.description: The Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the entity was last seen.

properties.description: A map of entity-type-specific properties. Available properties vary by entity type. For example, a HOST entity may include osType, cpuCores, and memoryTotalBytes.

tags.description: The list of tags applied to the entity, including auto-detected and manually defined tags.

items.description: A tag applied to a monitored entity.

context.description: The origin context of the tag. For example, CONTEXTLESS, ENVIRONMENT, AWS, KUBERNETES, etc.

value.description: The value of the tag, if applicable.

stringRepresentation.description: The full string representation of the tag as displayed in the Dynatrace UI, e.g., [KUBERNETES]app:my-service.

managementZones.description: The management zones that the entity belongs to, used for access control and organizational scoping.

id.description: The unique identifier of the management zone.

name.description: The display name of the management zone.

toRelationships.description: Relationships where this entity is the target. Keys are relationship types (e.g., isProcessOf), values are arrays of entity IDs. For example, {"isProcessOf": ["PROCESS_GROUP-123"]}.

fromRelationships.description: Relationships where this entity is the source. Keys are relationship types (e.g., runsOn), values are arrays of entity IDs. For example, {"runsOn": ["HOST-456"]}.
